# conwrt — Full Documentation for LLMs

> Safety-first OpenWrt router flashing and device discovery tooling. conwrt flashes firmware onto real hardware with hardware validation, AI-assisted discovery for new devices, and automated flashing for known ones.

This file contains the full content of conwrt's key documentation, inlined for single-fetch ingestion by AI systems. Source: https://github.com/Amperstrand/conwrt

---

## SAFETY RULES (from AGENTS.md — READ FIRST)

These rules are safety-critical. Violating them can brick real hardware.

### NEVER Force Sysupgrade

**`sysupgrade -F` is FORBIDDEN.** No exceptions.

The `-F` flag bypasses hardware validation and can brick devices by writing firmware for the wrong hardware. If sysupgrade rejects an image with "Device X not supported by this image", the image is wrong for the hardware. STOP.

Do NOT:
- Use `sysupgrade -F` or `--force`
- Override hardware validation checks
- Assume board.json is accurate (it reflects firmware, not hardware)

Instead:
- Investigate why the identity doesn't match
- Use `cat /tmp/sysinfo/board_name` to check device identity
- Download the correct firmware for the actual hardware
- If hardware identity is ambiguous, ask the operator before proceeding

### Always Identify Before Flashing

1. SSH to the device and read board.json AND `/tmp/sysinfo/board_name`
2. Cross-reference with the operator's claimed device model
3. If there's a mismatch, STOP and report it to the operator
4. Never assume the operator's claim is correct if the device says otherwise

### Trust Hardware Checks Over Firmware State

- `board.json` = what firmware was flashed (can be wrong)
- `/tmp/sysinfo/board_name` = firmware's device identity (can be wrong)
- `sysupgrade` validation = authoritative hardware check (trust this)
- Physical labels / MAC OUI = ground truth

### Test Before You Commit

Any shell script that modifies device state (uci set, network config, IP changes) must be tested end-to-end via SSH before being committed to automated flows.

1. SSH manually first: Run the exact shell commands on the device
2. Read back the value: After `uci set`, run `uci get` to confirm
3. Test persistence: Reboot and verify the change survives
4. Verify recovery: Confirm you know how to undo the change
5. Then automate: Only after steps 1-4 pass

Specific rules:
- Never `uci commit` a network IP change without verifying `uci get` returns the expected value first
- Shell variables in uci commands MUST use double quotes (not single quotes) for expansion
- On-device shell scripts use BusyBox tools only (`md5sum`, not `sha256sum`; no `chpasswd`, no `hostname`)
- Python-side hash algorithms MUST match what BusyBox provides (md5, not sha256)
- For UBIFS overlay devices: `uci commit` is permanent after reboot — there is no "undo" after reboot

### CI Rules

- `make ci` must not touch real routers (no SSH, ASU, TFTP, sysupgrade in tests)
- Mock network/hardware; use fixtures under `tests/` and `examples/`

---

## CLI REFERENCE

### Main CLI: conwrt.py

```bash
python3 scripts/conwrt.py [--version]
                          {flash,list,list-use-cases,cache,setup-mgmt-wifi,backup,
                           fingerprint,auto,configure,profile,reset,probe,flow} ...
```

**Subcommands:**

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `flash` | Flash firmware to device (default if no subcommand given) |
| `list` | List available device models |
| `list-use-cases` | List available use case presets |
| `cache` | Manage cached firmware images |
| `configure` | Apply config.toml settings to a running OpenWrt router via SSH |
| `profile` | Inspect operator profile (config.toml) plans |
| `probe` | Probe an interface for connected routers |
| `fingerprint` | Fingerprint a device to identify its model |
| `auto` | Auto-detect connected router and offer to flash it |
| `reset` | Factory reset an OpenWrt router (SSH firstboot or failsafe mode) |
| `backup` | Backup MTD flash partitions from a stock ZyXEL router via SSH |
| `flow` | Deploy or generate a composite flow (e.g. net4sats) |

### Flash command options

```
python3 scripts/conwrt.py flash \
  --model-id MODEL_ID          # Model ID from models/ (auto-detected if OpenWrt running)
  --image IMAGE                # Path to firmware image (vanilla or custom)
  --request-image              # Request custom image from ASU with baked-in settings
  --ssh-key SSH_KEY            # Path to SSH public key
  --password PASSWORD          # Set root password (default: random, printed once)
  --no-password                # Skip password (key-only auth)
  --wan-ssh                    # Open SSH on WAN port (requires --ssh-key)
  --interface INTERFACE        # Ethernet interface (auto-detected)
  --no-voice                   # Disable voice guidance
  --no-upload                  # Stop after detecting U-Boot (dry run)
  --no-pcap                    # Disable pcap monitoring (polling-only mode)
  --force-uboot                # Force U-Boot recovery mode even if OpenWrt detected
  --capture PATH               # Save pcap capture to file
  --flash-method METHOD        # Force specific flash method (auto-detected if omitted)
  --yes                        # Skip destructive-operation confirmations
```

### Common workflows

```bash
# List all supported devices
python3 scripts/conwrt.py list

# List all use case presets
python3 scripts/conwrt.py list-use-cases

# Auto-detect connected router
python3 scripts/conwrt.py auto

# Probe an interface for devices
python3 scripts/conwrt.py probe --json

# Fingerprint a device at a known IP
python3 scripts/conwrt.py fingerprint 192.168.1.1 --json

# Preview what config.toml would apply (dry-run)
python3 scripts/conwrt.py profile plan --model-id dlink-covr-x1860-a1

# Flash with a prebuilt image
python3 scripts/conwrt.py flash --model-id dlink-covr-x1860-a1 --image firmware.bin

# Request custom ASU image (bakes SSH key + config), then flash
python3 scripts/conwrt.py flash --model-id dlink-covr-x1860-a1 \
  --request-image --ssh-key ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub --no-password --wan-ssh

# Dry-run: detect recovery mode, skip upload
python3 scripts/conwrt.py flash --model-id dlink-covr-x1860-a1 --image fw.bin --no-upload

# Apply config.toml to a running router
python3 scripts/conwrt.py configure --ip 192.168.1.1
python3 scripts/conwrt.py configure --dry-run --ip 192.168.1.1

# Request custom ASU image only (no flash)
python3 scripts/firmware-manager.py request --profile dlink_covr-x1860-a1 --dry-run
```

---

## SUPPORTED DEVICES (21 models)

| Model ID | Vendor | Target | Flash Methods |
|----------|--------|--------|---------------|
| asus-lyra-map-ac2200 | ASUS | ipq40xx/generic | stock-ssh-mtd, sysupgrade, wifi-sta-flash |
| dlink-covr-x1860-a1 | D-Link | ramips/mt7621 | recovery-http, sysupgrade, dlink-hnap |
| extreme-networks-ws-ap3915i | Extreme Networks | ipq40xx/generic | extreme-rdwr-tftp-initramfs, sysupgrade, tftp |
| glinet-gl-ar150 | GL.iNet | ath79/generic | sysupgrade |
| glinet-gl-ar300m-lite | GL.iNet | ath79/generic | recovery-http, sysupgrade |
| glinet-gl-ar300m-nand | GL.iNet | ath79/nand | recovery-http, sysupgrade |
| glinet-gl-ar300m-nor | GL.iNet | ath79/nand | recovery-http, sysupgrade |
| glinet-mt3000 | GL.iNet | mediatek/filogic | uboot-http, sysupgrade |
| glinet-mt6000 | GL.iNet | mediatek/filogic | uboot-http, sysupgrade |
| linksys-mx4200-v1 | Linksys | qualcommax/ipq807x | sysupgrade, linksys-jnap-cve |
| linksys-whw03-v1 | Linksys | ipq40xx/generic | sysupgrade |
| linksys-whw03-v2 | Linksys | ipq40xx/generic | sysupgrade |
| ubnt-edgerouter-6p | Ubiquiti | octeon/generic | edgeos-kernel-swap, sysupgrade, tftp |
| ubnt-edgerouter-x | Ubiquiti | ramips/mt7621 | sysupgrade |
| zyxel-ex5700-telenor | Zyxel | mediatek/filogic | sysupgrade |
| zyxel-gs1900-24e | Zyxel | realtek/rtl838x | oem-http, sysupgrade, mtd-write, serial-tftp-openwrt |
| zyxel-gs1900-8hp-a1 | Zyxel | realtek/rtl838x | oem-http, sysupgrade, mtd-write, serial-tftp-openwrt |
| zyxel-gs1900-8hp-b1 | Zyxel | realtek/rtl838x | oem-http, sysupgrade, mtd-write, serial-tftp-openwrt |
| zyxel-gs1920-24 | Zyxel | realtek/rtl839x | oem-ftp, sysupgrade, serial-tftp-openwrt |
| zyxel-nr7101 | Zyxel | ramips/mt7621 | zycast, sysupgrade |
| zyxel-wx5600-t0 | Zyxel | mediatek/filogic | serial-tftp-openwrt, serial-tftp-stock-restore, sysupgrade |

---

## USE CASE PRESETS (21 presets)

Each preset is a Python file in `scripts/use_cases/` declaring packages + first-boot uci config. Enable in `config.toml`:

```toml
[use_cases]
enabled = ["tether", "sqm", "wireguard-client"]
```

| Preset | Description | Status | Requires |
|--------|-------------|--------|----------|
| tether | Auto-detect Android or iPhone USB WAN | tested | usb |
| tether-android | USB WAN from Android phone | tested | usb |
| tether-android-adb | USB WAN + ADB auto-enable | tested | usb |
| tether-ios | USB WAN from iPhone | tested | usb |
| sqm | Smart Queue Management with CAKE | tested | - |
| auto-sqm | Auto-measure WAN speed + SQM | experimental | - |
| wireguard-client | WireGuard VPN client | tested | - |
| wireguard-server | WireGuard VPN server | tested | - |
| adguard | Network-wide ad blocking | tested | - |
| guest-wifi | Isolated guest WiFi | untested | wifi |
| travelmate | Auto-connect captive portal WiFi | untested | wifi |
| mwan3 | Multi-WAN failover/load balancing | untested | - |
| doh | DNS-over-HTTPS | untested | - |
| mesh11sd | 802.11s mesh networking | experimental | wifi |
| openclash | Transparent proxy (Clash/Mihomo) | tested | - |
| tollgate | Bitcoin/Lightning payment gateway | tested | - |
| ssh-hardening | Dropbear hardening | untested | - |
| ssl | Trusted HTTPS certs for LAN | experimental | ethernet |
| ap-nostr-id | Nostr keypair per AP | experimental | ethernet |
| fips-bluetooth-rfcomm | FIPS mesh over Bluetooth | experimental | bluetooth |
| configurationwizzard | net4sats admin UI | experimental | - |

---

## CONFIGURATION (config.toml)

config.toml is the operator profile — gitignored, stays local. Copy from config.example.toml.

```toml
[ssh]
# Public keys for the router's authorized_keys.
# First key is also used for ASU image builds.
keys = ["~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub"]

[password]
# "random" = generate random password, print once (default)
# "key-only" = no password, SSH key auth only
# "<literal>" = use this exact string
mode = "random"

[asu]
extra_packages = ["luci", "luci-app-attendedsysupgrade"]

[network]
# lan_ip = "192.168.1.1"        # Router LAN IP (when set, conwrt changes it post-flash)
wan_ssh = false                  # Allow SSH on WAN interface
# mgmt_wifi = true               # Hidden management WiFi AP
# mgmt_wifi_txpower = 5          # dBm override

# WiFi STA (client mode — connect to upstream AP)
# [network.sta]
# band = "5ghz"                  # 2.4ghz, 5ghz, 5ghz-low, 5ghz-high
# ssid = "UpstreamNetwork"
# encryption = "psk2"            # psk2, psk3, sae, none
# key = "passphrase"

# WiFi AP (access point mode)
# [network.ap]
# band = "2.4ghz"
# ssid = "MyOpenWrt"
# encryption = "psk2"
# key = "passphrase"

# Use case presets
# [use_cases]
# enabled = ["tether", "sqm"]
#
# [use_cases.sqm]
# download_kbps = 340000         # 85-95% of actual download speed
# upload_kbps = 19000            # 85-95% of actual upload speed
```

---

## INTERNAL PYTHON API

Scripts use `sys.path.insert(0, scripts_dir)` for imports. Hyphenated filenames (`firmware-manager.py`, `router-fingerprint.py`) are NOT importable by name — use `importlib.import_module()` or `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location()`.

### Model Management (model_loader.py)

```python
from model_loader import list_models, load_model, find_model_by_board_name

models = list_models()                          # list[dict] — all 21 models
model = load_model("dlink-covr-x1860-a1")       # dict — single model definition
model = find_model_by_board_name("dlink,covr-x1860-a1")  # dict|None
```

### Use Case Registry (use_cases/__init__.py)

```python
from use_cases import registry, get, apply_defaults

ucs = registry()                    # dict[str, UseCase] — all presets
uc = get("tether")                  # UseCase|None — single preset
params = apply_defaults("sqm", {"download_kbps": 340000})  # dict — merged with defaults
```

### Device Detection

```python
# Fingerprint via SSH (router-fingerprint.py — hyphenated, use importlib)
import importlib
fp = importlib.import_module("router-fingerprint")
result = fp.fingerprint_router("192.168.1.1")   # dict|None — full fingerprint

# Probe boot state (router-probe.py — hyphenated)
rp = importlib.import_module("router-probe")
state = rp.probe_router(interface="en0", ip="192.168.1.1")  # RouterState

# Auto-detect (auto_detect.py)
from auto_detect import auto_detect
routers = auto_detect(interface="en0", passive_timeout=10)  # list[DetectedRouter]
```

### SSH Utilities (ssh_utils.py)

```python
from ssh_utils import run_ssh, check_ssh

result = run_ssh("192.168.1.1", "cat /etc/openwrt_release")  # CompletedProcess
reachable = check_ssh("192.168.1.1")                          # bool
```

### Inventory (inventory.py)

```python
from inventory import read_inventory, append_to_inventory, find_device

entries = read_inventory()                         # list[dict] — all devices
find_device(serial="ABC123")                       # dict|None
append_to_inventory({"mac": "...", "model": "..."})  # None
```

### Model JSON Structure

```json
{
  "id": "dlink-covr-x1860-a1",
  "vendor": "D-Link",
  "description": "COVR-X1860 A1 mesh WiFi",
  "openwrt": {
    "target": "ramips/mt7621",
    "device": "dlink,covr-x1860-a1",
    "profile": "dlink_covr-x1860-a1",
    "version": "24.10.2"
  },
  "hardware": {
    "soc": "MT7621AT",
    "flash": "NAND 128MB",
    "ram": "256MB"
  },
  "capabilities": ["wifi", "ethernet"],
  "mac_oui": ["BC:22:28"],
  "flash_methods": {
    "recovery-http": { "description": "U-Boot HTTP recovery", "recovery_ip": "192.168.0.1" },
    "sysupgrade": { "description": "Standard sysupgrade over SSH" }
  },
  "tested_hardware": {
    "recovery-http": { "tested": true, "date": "2026-05-16" }
  }
}
```

---

## FLASH METHODS

| Method | How it works | Requires | Speed |
|--------|-------------|----------|-------|
| sysupgrade | SSH + SCP, runs sysupgrade -n | Running OpenWrt + SSH | ~3 min |
| recovery-http | Reset button, uboot HTTP server | Physical access + reset pin | ~2 min |
| dlink-hnap | HNAP SOAP API upload via stock web UI | Stock D-Link firmware | BLOCKED on COVR-X1860 |
| tftp | TFTP server for uboot network boot | Serial or uboot access | varies |
| extreme-rdwr-tftp | SSH to stock → rdwr_boot_cfg → TFTP → sysupgrade | Stock Extreme firmware, SSH | ~5 min |
| zycast | Multicast to many devices | Network broadcast domain | varies |

---

## FILE STRUCTURE

```
conwrt/
├── llms.txt                    # This file's slim index version
├── llms-full.txt               # This file (full content)
├── README.md                   # Full project documentation
├── AGENTS.md                   # Safety rules for AI agents (READ FIRST)
├── CONTRIBUTING.md             # Contribution guidelines
├── config.example.toml         # Annotated config template
├── pyproject.toml              # Python project config (deps, ruff, pyright)
├── Makefile                    # CI targets: lint, typecheck, validate-models, test, ci, ipk
├── models/                     # 21 device model JSON files (species-level, static)
├── schemas/                    # JSON Schema for model validation
├── scripts/
│   ├── conwrt.py               # Main CLI dispatcher
│   ├── conwrt/                 # CLI package (cli.py, cmd_*.py, flash/, profile/)
│   ├── router-fingerprint.py   # SSH device fingerprinting
│   ├── router-probe.py         # Boot state detection
│   ├── firmware-manager.py     # ASU firmware build/download/cache
│   ├── model_loader.py         # Shared model registry reader
│   ├── use_cases/              # 21 use case preset plugins
│   ├── generate_matrix.py      # Generates docs/index.html (device matrix + landing page)
│   ├── generate_site.py        # Generates docs/wizard-bundle.json
│   ├── ssh_utils.py            # SSH execution helpers
│   ├── inventory.py            # Device inventory (specimen-level)
│   ├── tftp-server.py          # Bundled TFTP server (no dnsmasq dep)
│   └── platform_utils.py       # Platform detection (darwin/linux/openwrt)
├── docs/                       # GitHub Pages site (auto-deployed)
│   ├── index.html              # Landing page + device matrix
│   ├── wizard.html             # Interactive deployment wizard
│   ├── user-stories.html       # 5 persona-based stories
│   ├── getting-started.html    # Step-by-step guide
│   ├── process.md              # AI discovery workflow
│   ├── gotchas.md              # Device-specific quirks
│   ├── testing-status.md       # Hardware testing status
│   └── design-ops-pipeline.md  # Ops pipeline architecture
├── prompts/                    # AI discovery step templates (Stage 1)
├── recipes/                    # Per-device procedures and notes
├── examples/                   # Example artifacts (redacted)
├── tests/                      # Python unit tests + smoke tests
└── data/                       # Runtime data (gitignored)
    ├── inventory.jsonl          # Append-only device inventory
    └── *.bin                    # Cached firmware images
```

---

## RUNNING ON OPENWRT (Router-to-Router)

conwrt can run FROM an OpenWrt router to flash another router:

```bash
# On the host OpenWrt router:
opkg install python3-base python3-light python3-urllib python3-json \
  python3-codecs python3-ctypes python3-email python3-logging \
  python3-openssl python3-struct python3-fcntl curl

# Copy conwrt to the router (use scp -O — dropbear has no sftp-server)
scp -O -r scripts/ root@<host-ip>:/tmp/conwrt/
scp -O models/<model>.json root@<host-ip>:/tmp/conwrt/models/
scp -O firmware.bin root@<host-ip>:/tmp/conwrt/

# Run from the router
ssh root@<host-ip>
cd /tmp/conwrt/scripts
python3 conwrt.py --model-id dlink-covr-x1860-a1 \
  --image /tmp/conwrt/firmware.bin --no-pcap --no-voice
```

The interface is auto-detected as `br-lan` on OpenWrt. Use `--no-pcap` for polling-only mode (no tcpdump needed).

---

## OFFLINE DEVICE PACKAGE INSTALLATION

When a router has no internet access, use `scp -O` to transfer .ipk files:

```bash
# Download on host (with internet)
REPO_BASE="https://downloads.openwrt.org/releases/24.10.2/targets/mediatek/filogic"
PACKAGES_BASE="https://downloads.openwrt.org/releases/24.10.2/packages/aarch64_cortex-a53"
curl -O "${PACKAGES_BASE}/packages/kmod-usb-net-rndis_*.ipk"

# Transfer to router (scp -O required — dropbear has no sftp-server)
scp -O -i ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 *.ipk root@<router-ip>:/tmp/

# Install on router (dependencies first, or use wildcard)
ssh root@<router-ip> "opkg install /tmp/*.ipk"
```

Key: Always use `scp -O` — OpenWrt dropbear lacks `/usr/libexec/sftp-server`.

---

## EXTERNAL REPOSITORY ETIQUETTE

Never contribute to, comment on, or file issues against repositories outside the Amperstrand organization. This includes starring, fork-sync PRs, issue comments, and discussion posts. Reference external repos for research only.

## Related Projects

- **realtek-poe fork**: Amperstrand/realtek-poe — AI experimentation workspace for PoE research on OpenWrt switches. All AI work on the `ai-experiments` branch. Never interact with upstream Hurricos/realtek-poe.
